workflow_runs.name: Lint Check
workflow_runs.display_title: feat(desktop): distinguish processing/locked/recoverable from 'Untitled'

head_commit.message:
fix(desktop): greptile review — replace conversation + analytics for reprocess

Addresses two comments on #7630:

- P1: reprocessing a .failed conversation only updated structured.title.
  Since displayState checks status before title, the 'Failed' pill and
  reprocess CTA stayed stuck on the row indefinitely and the user could
  keep firing reprocess calls. Fix by replacing the whole conversation
  with the refreshed payload (new replaceConversation method on AppState
  — also picks up the updated overview, action items, status). The
  untitledRecoverable path already worked because its status was already
  .completed.
- P2: the new one-tap reprocess path had no analytics. Existing
  conversationReprocessed signature requires an appId, so added a
  separate conversationReprocessedDefault event to keep the two funnels
  distinguishable in product metrics.

workflow_runs.name: Lint Check
workflow_runs.display_title: feat(desktop): distinguish processing/locked/recoverable from 'Untitled'

head_commit.message:
feat(desktop): distinguish processing/locked/recoverable from 'Untitled'

Conversations with no title were all rendered as the same 'Untitled
Conversation' string regardless of why — still processing, locked
behind a stale subscription, LLM silently failed, or just ambient
capture. Users had no way to tell which, and no inline path to
recover the recoverable cases.

- New ConversationDisplayState on ServerConversation collapses
  status/isLocked/structured.title into one of: titled, processing,
  locked, untitledRecoverable, untitledEmpty, failed.
- New ConversationStatusBadge pill renders the right cue inline next
  to the row/header title (Processing/Locked/Failed/Needs reprocess).
- Placeholder titles render in textTertiary so they read as secondary
  text instead of pretending to be a real title.
- Untitled-but-recoverable rows expose a one-tap reprocess affordance
  (inline hover button + context-menu entry) that calls the existing
  POST /v1/conversations/{id}/reprocess. Empty rows don't, to avoid
  burning LLM tokens on ambient/accidental capture.
- ConversationDetailView header swaps its bespoke status badge for the
  shared component so all four cases render consistently.

Tests cover the displayState heuristic across the six branches incl.
the locked-takes-precedence and recoverable-vs-empty edge cases.

head_commit.message:
feat: regenerate daily summary in app three-dots menu + backend endpoint (#7627)

## Summary
Users wanted a way to re-run a specific daily recap (LLM output,
conversations changed, etc.). Existing backend had a generation pipeline
but no clean user-facing endpoint — only the
`/daily-summary-settings/test` dev endpoint, which always stacks a new
doc and always sends a push notification.

## Backend
- `POST /v1/users/daily-summaries/{summary_id}/regenerate` — re-runs
`generate_comprehensive_daily_summary` for the existing summary's date
and overwrites the **same** Firestore doc.
- New `daily_summaries_db.update_daily_summary` helper. The generator
always allocates a fresh UUID; this pins it back to `summary_id` so
readers keying off `summary['id']` keep finding the same row.
- Preserves `visibility` (don't reset sharing on regenerate); stamps
`regenerated_at`.
- 30s per-summary Redis cooldown — absorbs accidental double-taps
without burning LLM tokens. Returns 429 with a friendly message when
hit.
- No push notification: user is already looking at the page.
- Unit test covers the id-pinning behavior (most regression-prone bit).

## App
- Adds "Regenerate recap" to the existing three-dots action sheet on the
daily summary detail page (Cupertino sheet on iOS, Material bottom sheet
elsewhere). The code at the existing sheet already had a TODO comment
anticipating this.
- Tapping → blocking spinner → swaps page content with the refreshed
summary in place. No list re-fetch.
- Distinguishes the three backend failure modes in user-facing
messaging: 429 cooldown, 400 (no conversations for that day), or generic
retry.
- New API helper `regenerateDailySummary(summaryId)` returns a
structured result so the UI can render the right error.

## l10n
5 new keys translated across all 49 locales: `regenerateRecap`,
`recapRegeneratedSnackbar`, `recapRegenerateFailed`,
`recapRegenerateCooldown`, `recapRegenerateNoConversations`. Generated
dart files refreshed via `flutter gen-l10n`.

## Test plan
- [x] Backend unit test passes (`pytest
tests/unit/test_daily_summary_regenerate.py`)
- [x] `flutter analyze` clean on changed files (pre-existing warnings
unrelated)
- [ ] In app: open a recap → three-dots → Regenerate → spinner → content
updates
- [ ] Tap Regenerate twice rapidly → second attempt shows cooldown
snackbar
- [ ] Regenerate a recap whose source conversations were since deleted →
"no conversations" snackbar, original recap stays as-is
- [ ] Regenerate a recap that was shared → it stays shared (visibility
preserved)

workflow_runs.name: PR Declined Comment
workflow_runs.display_title: feat: regenerate daily summary in app three-dots menu + backend endpoint

head_commit.message:
fix(recap): greptile review — preserve created_at, race + lint

Addresses 4 comments on #7627:

- backend P1: preserve original created_at on regenerate. Generator
  always stamps a fresh utcnow(); without preserving it the UI's
  creation-time sorting would show the regen time, not when the
  recap was first created.
- backend P1: set the cooldown cache entry BEFORE the LLM call, not
  after. Two concurrent requests could both pass the check then both
  fire generation — eager set closes the practical race for
  accidental double-taps.
- backend P2: drop unused f-string prefix on the 429 detail.
- app P1: capture the rootNavigator before the await and dismiss the
  spinner unconditionally. If the widget unmounts mid-flight (route
  popped externally, OS kills activity), the prior mounted-check-
  before-pop would orphan the dialog with no way to clear it.

workflow_runs.name: Lint Check
workflow_runs.display_title: fix name speaker sheet setLoading setState after dismount crash (#7625)

head_commit.message:
fix name speaker sheet setLoading setState after dismount crash (#7625)

## Crash

`_NameSpeakerBottomSheetState.setLoading`

**Error:** `FlutterError - Null check operator used on a null value`

`package:omi/pages/conversation_detail/widgets/name_speaker_sheet.dart:46`

**Crashlytics:**
https://console.firebase.google.com/u/0/project/based-hardware/crashlytics/app/ios:com.friend-app-with-wearable.ios12/issues/7bdba9d5e1a0c447879ff76136832aa5?time=7d&types=crash&sessionEventKey=c58779409a924ba5a2bf195c09125e1a_2223379907360506788

**Logs:**
```
Fatal Exception: FlutterError
0  ???  0x0 State.setState + 1219 (framework.dart:1219)
1  ???  0x0 _NameSpeakerBottomSheetState.setLoading + 49 (name_speaker_sheet.dart:49)
2  ???  0x0 _NameSpeakerBottomSheetState._buildSaveButton.<fn> + 458 (name_speaker_sheet.dart:458)
```

## Fix

`setLoading(false)` is called after `await
widget.onSpeakerAssigned(...)` in the save button callback. If the
bottom sheet is dismissed while that async operation is in flight, the
widget is unmounted and Flutter's `setState` crashes on its internal
`_element!` null check. Added `if (!mounted) return;` in `setLoading`
before the `setState` call so it safely no-ops when the widget is
already gone.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

workflow_runs.name: Lint Check
workflow_runs.display_title: feat(phone): support clipboard paste on dialpad number display (#7626)

head_commit.message:
feat(phone): support clipboard paste on dialpad number display (#7626)

## Summary
- Phone dialer's keypad tab had no way to paste a number — only
digit-by-digit tap entry.
- Long-press on the number display now shows a Paste popup. Tap → pastes
clipboard content into the dialpad.
- Pasted text is sanitized to keep `0-9`, `*`, `#`, and a leading `+`.
Strips spaces, dashes, parens, dots, etc., so `+1 (415) 555-1234`
becomes `+14155551234`.

## Notes
- Reuses existing `context.l10n.paste` key (already translated in all 49
ARBs from the vocabulary settings flow), so no l10n changes.
- Long-press is consistent with the existing convention on this screen
(backspace button's long-press = clear-all).
- Haptic feedback: light tap on long-press, medium on successful paste.

## Test plan
- [ ] Copy "+1 (415) 555-1234" to clipboard, open dialer keypad,
long-press the number display → "Paste" menu appears → tap → field shows
"+14155551234"
- [ ] Copy a plain digits-only number → same flow → digits appear as-is
- [ ] Copy text with no digits ("hello world") → long-press → menu does
not appear (sanitized empty)
- [ ] Empty clipboard → long-press → no menu
- [ ] Paste replaces any existing typed digits (matches iOS dialer
behavior)

workflow_runs.name: Lint Check
workflow_runs.display_title: i18n(android): localize notification channel name and description

head_commit.message:
i18n(android): localize notification channel name and description

The Android notification channel name and description appear in the OS
settings UI (Settings → Apps → Omi → Notifications). They were
previously hardcoded English strings, so Japanese users always saw
"Omi Notifications" / "Notification channel for Omi" /
"Foreground Service Notification" there even with the app set to
Japanese.

Why this can't simply use `context.l10n`:

`awesome_notifications` and `flutter_foreground_task` register
channels eagerly — sometimes from `_firebaseMessagingBackgroundHandler`
(a `@pragma('vm:entry-point')` callback) and from static instance
fields — both before any `BuildContext` or even the widgets binding
is available. We therefore read `Platform.localeName` directly.

Changes:

- New file: `app/lib/utils/notification_channel_strings.dart` — a
  tiny dependency-free helper that picks Japanese or English based on
  `Platform.localeName`. Only Japanese is added for now; every other
  locale falls through to the existing English text, so non-JA users
  see no change.

- Four call sites updated to read from the helper (3-line patch each:
  one new import, two string substitutions):
  - `lib/main.dart` — background FCM channel.
  - `lib/services/notifications/notification_service_basic.dart` —
    Windows/local-only channel.
  - `lib/services/notifications/notification_service_fcm.dart` —
    iOS/Android/macOS/web/Linux FCM channel.
  - `lib/utils/audio/foreground.dart` — foreground-service channel
    used during transcription.

Strings now localized for Japanese:
- "Omi Notifications" → "Omi の通知"
- "Notification channel for Omi" → "Omi の通知チャンネル"
- "Foreground Service Notification" → "フォアグラウンドサービス通知"
- "Transcription service is running in the background."
  → "文字起こしサービスがバックグラウンドで実行中です。"

The OS only re-reads the channel name/description on first registration,
so existing installs will see the localized text after the channel is
re-created (e.g. reinstall or app data clear); this matches Android's
standard behavior for channel metadata changes.

workflow_runs.name: Lint Check
workflow_runs.display_title: i18n(ja): improve translation quality and terminology consistency

head_commit.message:
i18n(ja): improve translation quality and terminology consistency

Cleans up translation quality issues in app_ja.arb (and the matching
generated app_localizations_ja.dart). All changes are in-place value
edits — no keys added or removed, no behavior change for other locales.

Fixes (38 keys):

1. Untranslated English remnants (5)
   - on: 'On' → 'オン'
   - chatQuotaSubtitle: 'AI chat messages used with Omi this month.'
                      → '今月 Omi で使用した AI チャットメッセージ数。'
   - appName:      'App Name' → 'アプリ名'
   - appIconLabel: 'App Icon' → 'アプリアイコン'
   - mindMap:      'Mind Map' → 'マインドマップ'

2. Terminology consistency: 'スピーカー' → '話者' (1)
   Other 10 speaker-related strings already used '話者'. The only
   outlier was autoCreateSpeakers, now aligned. phoneSpeaker is left
   as 'スピーカー' because there it refers to the phone's speakerphone
   hardware, not to a person speaking.

3. Terminology consistency: 'オーディオ' → '音声' (30)
   Existing strings used '音声' in 58 places and 'オーディオ' in 30
   places. Unified on the majority spelling '音声' across all 30
   call sites (audio input/output, system audio capture, audio bytes,
   audio files, etc.). Also normalized incidental spacing
   ('SDカード' → 'SD カード', 'Omiは' → 'Omi は').
   storeAudioOnPhone additionally fixes '電話に' → 'スマートフォンに'
   since Omi is a phone app, not a landline app.

4. Unnatural word order / direct translation (2)
   - chatUsageMessages: 'チャット: {used} / {limit} 件の今月のメッセージ'
                      → '今月のチャット: {used} / {limit} 件'
   - premiumMinutesMonth: '月間600プレミアム分。…'
                        → '月間 600 分のプレミアム枠。「オンデバイス」タブでは
                          無料の無制限文字起こしが利用できます。'

Out of scope / intentionally kept as-is:
- pro / txtFormat / pdfFormat: brand or file-extension labels.
- 33 brand/protocol strings (Omi, Bluetooth, PayPal, Stripe, GitHub,
  MCP, etc.) where the English form is the convention.

repository.name: omi
repository.description: AI that sees your screen, listens to your conversations and tells you what to do
